channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: The US is hunting for eggs in Europe
item.description: It is common in parts of Europe for consumers to buy eggs that still have feathers and chicken poop stuck to them.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: What to know about Boulder, Colorado, the Sundance Film Festival's new home
item.description: The Sundance Film Festival is Boulder bound, leaving its home of four decades in Park City, Utah, for a new chapter in neighboring Colorado. Organizers announced their decision Thursday after a yearlong search in which numerous U.S.
item.dc:creator: HANNAH SCHOENBAUM and MEAD GRUVER, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Massachusetts mayor who shuttered a libelous newspaper faces renewed corruption allegations
item.description: EVERETT, Mass. (AP) — For years, the mayor of a Boston suburb dreaded Wednesdays. That was the day when a local weekly would publish shocking allegations that he was on the take, sexually harassing women or under investigation by the FBI.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL CASEY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Turkish student at Tufts University is latest Palestinian supporter swept up in US crackdown
item.description: BOSTON (AP) — A Turkish student detained by federal officers as she walked along a street in a Boston suburb is the latest supporter of Palestinian causes to be swept up in the Trump administration’s crackdown on immigrants who express their political views.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL CASEY, JAKE OFFENHARTZ and KATHY McCORMACK, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Another federal judge blocks Trump policy banning transgender troops in the military
item.description: TACOMA, Wash. (AP) — A U.S. judge in Washington state has blocked enforcement of President Donald Trump’s order banning transgender people from serving in the military, the second nationwide injunction against the policy in as many weeks. The order Thursday from U.S.
item.dc:creator: GENE JOHNSON, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Luigi Mangione may get a laptop in jail to work on his defense in CEO murder case
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — A judge cleared the way Thursday for Luigi Mangione to get a laptop behind bars — if his jailers agree — so he can examine documents and other material in the UnitedHealthcare CEO murder case.
item.dc:creator: JENNIFER PELTZ and MICHAEL R. SISAK, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Mixed verdict for 3 Philadelphia detectives in perjury trial involving a 2016 murder exoneration
item.description: PHILADELPHIA (AP) — The trial of three long-retired Philadelphia detectives accused of lying about evidence in a homicide case that later ended with an exoneration wrapped up with a mixed verdict Thursday.
item.dc:creator: MARYCLAIRE DALE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Health and Human Services will lay off 10,000 workers in major restructuring
item.description: In a major overhaul, the U.S. Department of Health and Human Services will lay off 10,000 workers and shut down entire agencies, including ones that oversee billions of dollars in funds for addiction services and community health centers across the country.
item.dc:creator: Amanda Seitz, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Turkish student at Tufts University is latest Palestinian supporter detained by feds
item.description: A Turkish student detained by federal officers as she walked along a street in a Boston suburb is the latest supporter of Palestinian causes to be swept up in the Trump administration’s crackdown on immigrants who have expressed their political views.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL CASEY, JAKE OFFENHARTZ and KATHY McCORMACK Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Here are the international students and faculty known to be targeted by ICE
item.description: The Trump administration’s crackdown on foreign students and faculty who have voiced support for the Palestinian cause has escalated, with universities across the country seeing students either arrested by Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE) or having their visas revoked.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, Fox 5

item.title: Ulster County rejects Texas motion against New York abortion prescriber
item.description: Ulster County rejected a motion seeking a civil penalty of over $100,000 against Dr. Margaret Carpenter of New Paltz over a missed court appearance for prescribing mifepristone to a Texan.
item.dc:creator: Johan Sheridan, NewsNation

item.title: Tennessee father recalls being shot while holding two infants
item.description: "I was standing there with the girls at the driver window, and he already had a gun on me," he recalled.
item.dc:creator: Kori Johnson, NewsNation

item.title: Prosecutors in the killings of 4 Idaho students seek to use man's crime scene paper as evidence
item.description: Prosecutors in the case of a man charged in the killings of four University of Idaho students are seeking to use as evidence a college paper he wrote that shows his knowledge of crime scenes, according to a court filing this week Bryan Kohberger, 30, is accused in the stabbing deaths of Ethan Chapin
item.dc:creator: ADRIAN SAINZ, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: DC-bound Amtrak passenger may have exposed travelers to measles, health officials say
item.description: The passenger rode an Amtrak train in the Northeast last week before visiting an urgent care center in DC, health officials said.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, Fox 5

item.title: Judge: High heat in Texas prisons unconstitutional
item.description: A federal judge found the extreme heat in Texas prisons is “plainly unconstitutional” but did not order the state to begin installing air conditioners.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, Fox 5

item.title: Firefighters make progress on containing some Carolina wildfires, but others grow
item.description: Firefighters announced progress Wednesday night on containing two of the largest wildfires burning in the North Carolina mountains, but they warned that fire danger remained from dry and windy conditions.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: New Social Security clawback policy starts this week: What to know
item.description: A major change in how the Social Security Administration (SSA) treats its overpayments is set to kick in Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Tanner, Fox 5

item.title: Million-dollar lottery ticket was nearly thrown out, winner says
item.description: “I was actually kind of going to throw them away, because I said ‘I’ve never won the lottery,’” said Joaquin Vaughn Redo.
item.dc:creator: Travis Schlepp, Fox 5

item.title: 5 takeaways from House hearing on NPR, PBS
item.description: The heads of the nation's top public broadcasters faced an intense grilling from lawmakers Wednesday over their outlets’ funding and editorial objectivity.
item.dc:creator: Dominick Mastrangelo, Fox 5

item.title: Chewing gum releases thousands of microplastics into body, study finds
item.description: A recent study found that chewing gum can release up to thousands of microplastics per piece and potentially be ingested, according to researchers.
item.dc:creator: Rhea Caoile, NewsNation

item.title: 5 takeaways from House hearing on NPR, PBS
item.description: The heads of the nation's top public broadcasters faced an intense grilling from lawmakers Wednesday on Capitol Hill over their outlets’ funding and editorial objectivity.
item.dc:creator: Dominick Mastrangelo, NewsNation

item.title: Family unknowingly had rare, valuable painting for 150 years
item.description: A painting hanging in a French family's living room turned out to be far more valuable than just another trinket or heirloom.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, KRON4

item.title: Turkish student at Tufts University detained, video shows masked agents handcuffing her
item.description: Rumeysa Ozturk's whereabouts are unknown and no known charges have been filed against her, her lawyer said Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Trump places 25% tariff on imported autos, expecting to raise $100 billion in tax revenues
item.description: White House press secretary Karoline Leavitt said the tariffs would be detailed at a 4 p.m. EST news conference.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Kermit the Frog to give commencement address at University of Maryland
item.description: Prominent political figures and lawmakers on the commencement speaker circuit are about to have some friendly, froggy competition.
item.dc:creator: Judy Kurtz, WFLA, NBC

item.title: The MLB teams with the highest
item.description: A California team has the highest median ticket price, a new report found, but they don't play in Los Angeles.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, Fox 5

item.title: Retired immigration judge recalls unaccompanied infants in need of lawyers
item.description: A retired federal immigration judge on Tuesday spoke out against the Trump administration for canceling federal funds for lawyers to represent unaccompanied minors who illegally cross the border into the United States.
item.dc:creator: Sandra Sanchez, Fox 5

item.title: Family of Riley Strain files wrongful death lawsuit against Delta Chi fraternity
item.description: Family members of Riley Strain, a University of Missouri student who died last year during a group trip to Nashville, Tennessee, have filed a wrongful death lawsuit against his former fraternity and pledge brothers.
item.dc:creator: Joey Schneider, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Live updates: Ratcliffe, Gabbard pressed on war plans Signal group chat
item.description: The Trump administration is scrambling to contain the fallout from a bombshell Atlantic article revealing its editor-in-chief had been mistakenly included in a Signal group chat involving national security adviser Mike Waltz, Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth, intelligence leadership and other Trump ad
item.dc:creator: The Hill staff, Fox 5

item.title: You're an American in another land? Prepare to talk about the why and how of Trump 2.0
item.description: LONDON (AP) — The urgent care doctor cocked an eyebrow at Mari Santos and her American accent. It was four days after President Donald Trump's inauguration, and Santos was a student with a stomach bug in the first weeks of an overseas semester in Glasgow, Scotland.
item.dc:creator: LAURIE KELLMAN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Columbia student sues Trump admin over efforts to deport her
item.description: Columbia University student Yunseo Chung is suing the Trump administration amid its efforts to deport the 21-year-old, who has been in the U.S. since she was 7 and has lawful permanent resident status.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, ABC 4

item.title: Postmaster General Louis DeJoy resigns after 5 years in the position
item.description: The head of the beleaguered U.S. Postal Service, Louis DeJoy, resigned Monday after nearly five years in the position, leaving as President Donald Trump and Elon Musk's Department of Government Efficiency have floated the idea of privatizing mail service.
item.dc:creator: LISA BAUMANN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Judge blocks three agencies from disclosing troves of sensitive personal data to DOGE
item.description: A federal judge on Monday indefinitely blocked the Department of Government Efficiency's (DOGE) access to troves of sensitive personal data from three federal agencies, the latest roadblock to its efforts to slim down the federal bureaucracy.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, WGN 9

item.title: 23andMe files for bankruptcy amid financial struggles, CEO resignation
item.description: 23andMe has filed for bankruptcy and will be seeking to sell the ancestry company, a move that comes a month after it agreed to a settlement related to a 2023 data breach.
item.dc:creator: Joshua Hallenbeck, ABC 4

item.title: Young Michigan hockey player goes home after months of rehab
item.description: Months after a young hockey player checked in at Mary Free Bed Rehabilitation Hospital, it's safe to say he earned his tap-out.
item.dc:creator: Brittany Flowers, WGN 9
